title: taraxacum serotinum subsp. besarabicum
intro: Taraxacum serotinum subsp. besarabicum is a subspecies of dandelion, a perennial plant known for its yellow flowers and edible leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis, kidney stones, and digestive issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of vitamins A, C, and K, as well as its flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Taraxacum serotinum subsp. besarabicum can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or capsule, or used as a topical application to reap its health benefits.
